author: Marcus Chen
title: LLM SEO: How to Get ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini to Cite Your Brand
description: A new discipline is emerging alongside traditional SEO. The brands showing up in AI-generated answers are doing specific things to earn citations. Here's what they are.

sections.p: When someone asks ChatGPT 'what's the best AI marketing agency,' they're not getting a list of blue links. They're getting a synthesized answer that may or may not mention your brand. That answer is generated from training data, real-time web search, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation depending on the platform. Getting your brand into those answers is the new SEO — and it follows different rules.

sections.h2: How LLMs decide what to cite

sections.p: Large language models learn entity associations during training. If your brand is consistently mentioned alongside specific problem-solution pairs — 'NetWebMedia for AI marketing automation,' 'Jasper for AI copywriting' — that association becomes part of the model's embedded knowledge. Strong associations produce unsolicited citations. Weak or absent associations produce omission.

For real-time search-augmented models like Perplexity and ChatGPT with web access, the current web matters more. These systems retrieve and synthesize from live sources — so traditional AEO content structure (direct answers, clear claims, structured data) directly influences citation probability.

list: Define your category language precisely. Use the same 3–5 phrases to describe your category consistently across every piece of content, your website, press, and partner mentions.

Earn mentions on high-authority sources. LLMs over-index on sources like TechCrunch, Wired, Forbes, G2, and industry-specific publications. One mention on TechCrunch is worth a hundred on unknown blogs.
Publish direct-answer content. 'What is [your category]?' and 'How does [your approach] work?' pages — answered in 1–2 sentences at the top — feed directly into LLM synthesis.

Build entity consistency. Same name format, same description, same category claim across your website, LinkedIn, G2, Crunchbase, Wikipedia if applicable. LLMs use consistency to confirm entity identity.
Get reviewed. G2 and Capterra reviews appear in Perplexity and ChatGPT answers for product-evaluation queries. Recency matters — aim for 2+ new reviews per month.

sections.p: Traditional rank tracking doesn't work for LLM visibility. The measurement approach: define 20–30 queries where you want to be cited, run them weekly across ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini, and track citation rate. A brand appearing in 40% of relevant queries has meaningful LLM presence. Under 10% and you're essentially invisible to AI-native researchers.

Training data has a cutoff. For base-model citation in ChatGPT, content published today won't influence the model until the next training run — which may be 12–18 months away. For search-augmented models (Perplexity, ChatGPT with browsing), content published today can influence citations within days. Prioritize the search-augmented play now while building the training-data foundation for the long term.

sections.quote: LLM SEO isn't about gaming an algorithm. It's about being a genuinely authoritative, well-documented entity in your category. The models just notice.
